name: NIS2 Article 21 Endpoint Traceability Pack
license: EU legal references and short CSA-authored traceability labels only.

controls.title: Risk analysis and information system security policies
controls.notes: Endpoint data cannot establish legal or organizational compliance.

controls.title: Vulnerability handling and disclosure traceability

mappings.rationale: Known-vulnerability results provide endpoint-level supporting evidence for vulnerability handling.
evidenceLimitations: Does not assess disclosure, remediation governance, secure acquisition, or development lifecycle.

mappings.rationale: Update freshness is related operational context.
evidenceLimitations: Update recency alone does not demonstrate vulnerability management.

controls.notes: Traceability only; never a legal compliance determination.

controls.title: Access control and asset management traceability

mappings.rationale: Local administrator review supplies limited endpoint access-control evidence.
evidenceLimitations: Identity lifecycle, authorization governance, and non-endpoint assets are not assessed.

mappings.rationale: Software inventory quality is related asset-management context.
evidenceLimitations: Unknown software detection is not a complete asset inventory.

controls.notes: Traceability only; never a legal compliance determination.

controls.title: Authentication and secure communications measures

mappings.rationale: RDP Network Level Authentication is related endpoint context, not evidence of organization-wide MFA.
evidenceLimitations: MFA, continuous authentication, and secured communications policies are not assessed.

controls.notes: Contextual traceability only; manual organizational assessment is required.
